Event: Leela keeps the Planet Express ship flying competently as an ace pilot
Explanation: Her skill keeps herself employed/alive and directly protects and benefits the crew.

Event: Leela protects Fry in stand-up fights, with him cowering behind her
Explanation: She accepts personal risk to shield Fry while also affirming her role as capable fighter.

Event: Leela is tricked by Alcazar into an Adam-and-Eve scenario and almost marries him
Explanation: She is deceived into a limiting relationship that benefits Alcazar while undermining her own autonomy.

Event: Leela is again tricked by Zapp into an Adam-and-Eve scenario on “Edenic” Earth
Explanation: She is manipulated into a contrived romantic setup that flatters Zapp and compromises her standards.

Event: Leela admits Fry’s take-charge attitude during a mission turns her on
Explanation: Openly valuing his initiative strengthens their bond and encourages his growth.

Event: Leela dates several boyfriends despite social mockery of her love life
Explanation: She pursues relationships that meet her standards, gaining experience and connection without evident harm.

Event: Leela makes out with Amy in Cornwood
Explanation: Both gain mutual pleasure/experimentation with no shown negative fallout.

Event: Leela enters a romantic relationship with Yivo
Explanation: She engages in a mutually desired relationship with no stated harm.

Event: Leela becomes a blernsball player despite terrible aim and injures batters
Explanation: She gains fame and career benefits while repeatedly hurting others with wild pitches.

Event: Leela fawns over and protects animals, even dangerous ones
Explanation: Her devotion benefits creatures but often exposes her to danger and trouble.

Event: Leela obsessively hunts the space whale, risking her crew’s lives
Explanation: Her revenge-driven obsession endangers everyone, including herself, without clear benefit.

Event: Leela’s failure as first female blernsball player spurs women to sign up
Explanation: Her public failure hurts her reputation but motivates many women to prove themselves.

Event: Leela uses martial arts and direct confrontation to resolve many threats
Explanation: Her combat skills usually protect herself and others and end problems efficiently.

Event: Leela sometimes puts pride and grudges over crew safety as captain
Explanation: Letting ego override safety risks her life and the crew’s with no real gains.

Event: Leela kicks bullies who treat kids poorly
Explanation: She risks some conflict to defend children, discouraging abusive behavior.

Event: Leela is comfortable with Scruffy being injured after being distracted by her chest
Explanation: She passively accepts others’ harm that stems from her sexualized presence without concern.

Event: Leela keeps elbow talons trimmed
Explanation: She manages a mutation practically, reducing self-inconvenience and social/physical issues.

Event: Leela keeps a sentimental attachment to her orphanage basket
Explanation: Retaining the basket gives her emotional comfort without evident downside.

Event: Leela decides to overdose on space honey to sleep forever
Explanation: She moves toward self-destruction out of despair, mainly harming herself while sparing others further burden.

Event: Leela frequently dates men who later prove to be jerks
Explanation: Her poor romantic judgment mainly damages her emotionally while temporarily benefiting the men.

Event: Leela embarks on a long search for other one-eyed aliens
Explanation: The quest fulfills deep identity needs; although misled at points, the underlying pursuit is self-affirming.

Event: Leela allows Fry’s genuine love to soften her cynicism about romance
Explanation: Accepting Fry brings her lasting happiness and gives him the relationship he wants.

Event: Leela decides to swim in toxic waste, relying on her mutant immunity
Explanation: She uses an innate advantage to perform hazardous tasks safely.

Event: Leela chooses to keep her cyclops eye instead of permanent two-eyed surgery
Explanation: Embracing her natural eye improves her self-acceptance and preserves the uniqueness Fry values.

Event: Leela takes on command of the Earth fleet in Bender’s Big Score
Explanation: She gains authority and attempts to defend Earth, even though she later fails strategically.

Event: Leela takes Nibbler in as a pet due to her animal-love impulse
Explanation: She gives him care and companionship, and he later proves highly important.

Event: Leela brings a killer space bee aboard because it’s cute (dream plot)
Explanation: Her indulgent choice entertains her but results in Fry’s apparent death and great danger.

Event: Leela refuses to kill the Tunneling Horror to keep a vow not to kill
Explanation: Her absolutist ethics endanger everyone without yielding any clear positive outcome.

Event: Leela repeatedly protects a muck leech that keeps attacking her
Explanation: Her insistence on protecting it benefits the creature but leaves her hurt and exploited.

Event: Leela often refuses to accept responsibility for her errors
Explanation: Deflection protects her ego and standing while shifting costs and frustration onto others.

Event: Leela flies around for hours to avoid paying a nominal parking fee
Explanation: She wastes time and fuel, then lands in the La Brea Tar Pits, harming everyone’s interests.

Event: Leela neglects to vote in the crucial election she urged Fry to care about
Explanation: She spares herself minimal effort while contributing to a disastrous election outcome.

Event: Leela follows Fry and Bender into the army so they won’t get hurt
Explanation: She risks her own safety to guard them, improving their survival chances.

Event: Leela joins a Straw Feminist eco-terrorist group to oppose Leo Wong
Explanation: Terror tactics risk her and others without clearly effective environmental gains in the text.

Event: Leela searches obsessively for other one-eyed aliens and nearly marries Alcazar
Explanation: Her obsession to belong leads her into a manipulative relationship that mostly harms her.

Event: Leela embraces eco-activism to protect animals and nature
Explanation: Her environmental advocacy serves her values and tangibly benefits nonhuman life.

Event: Leela rubs off on Fry, encouraging him to take things more seriously
Explanation: Her influence improves Fry’s responsibility while giving her a more reliable partner.

Event: Leela sometimes escalates to violence as first resort when angered
Explanation: Acting on aggression vents her frustration but often hurts or intimidates others unnecessarily.

Event: Leela uses a shock collar yet continues hitting people, learning to enjoy shocks
Explanation: She persists in violence and gains perverse enjoyment while others remain targets and collateral.
